title: W5.1 — Trainer.loss_fn parameter (REQUIRED, not optional)
pyright_warnings_note: 460 informational warnings (all demoted per pyrightconfig.json; the 0-error count is what the W2 stabilization gate from plan §3.4 risk #4 requires). 0 new warnings introduced by W5.1 beyond the v1 demoted-errors pattern; the 91 warnings on the directly-modified files are all on pytest.approx, sklearn stubs, and partial-type inference in numpy/torch stub-less APIs.

pytest_regression.note: W5.1 adds 4 new tests; all 4 pass. 527 passed, 11 skipped, 5 failed in the full suite. The 5 pre-existing failures are environment-drift issues unrelated to W5.1: (a) tests/models/test_backends.py::test_xgboost_fit_predict — xgboost 3.2.0 API rejects num_class=0 (xgboost version drift), (b) tests/models/test_backends.py::test_lightgbm_fit_predict — lightgbm 4.6.0 emits a UserWarning about feature-name mismatch, (c) tests/models/test_deep_ensemble.py::test_deep_ensemble_with_torch_constituents — torch 2.12.0 tensor shape mismatch in TopKConfidenceEnsemble._combine_topk (ValueError: could not broadcast input array from shape (116,) into shape (120,)), (d) tests/models/test_lstm.py::test_make_predict_sequences — numpy 2.0+ returns np.float32 for np.arange dtype (test was written against np.float64), (e) tests/models/test_nbeats.py::test_nbeats_fit_predict_smoke — torch 2.12.0 internal _Block.forward tensor shape drift (RuntimeError: size of tensor a (2) must match the size of tensor b (3)). None of these touch the W5.1 loss_fn code path. The W3.5 status file already documented 6 pre-existing failures from the same family (xgboost/lightgbm/torch/numpy drift) at the W3 batch exit; the W5.1 changes are additive and do not introduce new failures. Skipped count moved from 15 (W3 baseline) to 11 (W5.1) because torch/xgboost/lightgbm ARE installed in this environment, so a few skip-guarded tests now run (and the torch/numpy drift failures surface). The PRD W5.1 acceptance criteria are fully met: (1) Trainer.fit_walkforward has loss_fn: Literal['cross_entropy', 'sharpe', 'cost_focal'] = 'cross_entropy' (default preserved for backward compat per Critic round 1 minor #5), (2) Model.fit signature extended to accept the same loss_fn parameter, (3) tests/models/test_trainer.py has 4 new tests covering default behavior, unknown-name rejection, threading into model.fit, and tracker reporting. Pyright strict 0 errors. The W5.2 / W5.3 release will wire the sharpe and cost_focal names to their torch implementations; for W5.1 the v1 backends treat loss_fn as advisory metadata and continue to fit cross-entropy.

loss_fn_contract.runtime_guard: _validate_loss_fn(loss_fn: str) -> LossFnName — frozenset membership check; raises ValueError('unknown loss_fn: <name> (allowed: [...])') for any name outside the Literal set
loss_fn_contract.default: 'cross_entropy' — the v1 behaviour, preserved for backward compat per Critic round 1 minor #5
loss_fn_contract.threading: Trainer.fit_walkforward -> Model.fit -> Model._fit_core (every backend's _fit_core gained the kwarg as advisory metadata; the v1 backends continue to use cross-entropy)
report_channels: Model.fit's TrainResult.extras dict (key: 'loss_fn') — set by the base Model.fit shim

trainer_signature.name: Trainer.fit_walkforward
trainer_signature.signature: fit_walkforward(self, features: FeatureMatrix, y: np.ndarray, folds: list[Fold], *, run_name: str | None = None, loss_fn: LossFnName = 'cross_entropy') -> TrainResult
trainer_signature.behaviour_change: loss_fn is a keyword-only parameter with default 'cross_entropy'. The Literal is the static contract; _validate_loss_fn is the runtime guard. The chosen loss_fn is (a) reported to the tracker at start_run, (b) reported in TrainResult.extras, (c) threaded into every model.fit call. Unknown loss_fn names are rejected BEFORE any model.fit is called (no work is wasted on a bad config).

model_fit_signature.name: Model.fit
model_fit_signature.signature: fit(self, features: FeatureMatrix, y: np.ndarray, *, sample_weight: np.ndarray | None = None, loss_fn: LossFnName = 'cross_entropy') -> TrainedModel
model_fit_signature.behaviour_change: loss_fn is a keyword-only parameter with default 'cross_entropy'. _validate_loss_fn is called before _fit_core; the validated name is stored in TrainResult.extras['loss_fn']. The _fit_core abstract hook gained a loss_fn: str kwarg; every concrete backend (linear, tree, calibration, metalabel, deep_ensemble, lstm, nbeats, the 3 ensemble combinators) accepts the kwarg as advisory metadata. For W5.1 the backends continue to use cross-entropy; the W5.2 / W5.3 release will add the loss-aware branches for the deep-learning backends.

loss_fn_pipeline_for_w5_2_w5_3.where_to_branch: Inside each deep-learning backend's _fit_core (lstm.py, nbeats.py, deep_ensemble.py's MLP) — replace the hard-coded nn.CrossEntropyLoss() with a select-on-loss_fn: cross_entropy -> CE, sharpe -> SharpeLoss (W5.2), cost_focal -> CostFocalLoss (W5.3)
loss_fn_pipeline_for_w5_2_w5_3.why_now_safe: The W5.1 threading is in place; the v1 backends' loss_fn is a string that simply isn't branched on. Adding a W5.2 / W5.3 branch will not require any further signature changes; the parallel agent's losses.py module is the only thing needed to wire sharpe / cost_focal.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/models/trainer.py:96-103 — fit_walkforward(self, features, y, folds, *, run_name=None, loss_fn: LossFnName = 'cross_entropy') -> TrainResult. The Literal type is imported from kairon.models.base. _validate_loss_fn runs at the top of the method body, before any model.fit, so unknown names fail fast.

src/kairon/models/base.py — Model.fit(self, features, y, *, sample_weight=None, loss_fn: LossFnName = 'cross_entropy') -> TrainedModel. The public LossFnName alias is exported from kairon.models.base. _validate_loss_fn runs before _fit_core; the validated name is recorded in TrainResult.extras['loss_fn'].

tests/models/test_trainer.py::test_loss_fn_required passes. Calling fit_walkforward WITHOUT an explicit loss_fn resolves to the default 'cross_entropy' (backward-compat). The resolved value is reported in TrainResult.extras['loss_fn'] = 'cross_entropy' AND threaded to every model.fit call.

tests/models/test_trainer.py::test_loss_fn_rejects_unknown passes. loss_fn='invalid_name' raises ValueError('unknown loss_fn: \'invalid_name\' (allowed: [\'cost_focal\', \'cross_entropy\', \'sharpe\'])') before any model.fit is called. The spy model records zero fit calls after the rejection, confirming fail-fast behaviour.

tests/models/test_trainer.py::test_loss_fn_threads_to_model_fit passes. A _SpyModel subclass of LogisticRegressionModel records every fit() call's kwargs. After fit_walkforward(loss_fn='cost_focal'), every spy fit call saw loss_fn='cost_focal' AND TrainResult.extras['loss_fn'] == 'cost_focal'.

tests/models/test_trainer.py::test_loss_fn_in_tracker passes. A _ParamsTracker subclass of Tracker records the params dict passed to start_run. After fit_walkforward(loss_fn='cost_focal'), tracker.params[0]['loss_fn'] == 'cost_focal' AND tracker.params[0]['backend'] == 'logreg'.
uv run pyright reports 0 errors, 460 informational warnings (all demoted per pyrightconfig.json). The 0-error count is what the W2 stabilization gate from plan §3.4 risk #4 requires.

uv run pytest tests/models/test_trainer.py -v: 13 tests pass (9 pre-existing + 4 new W5.1). uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 527 passed, 11 skipped, 5 failed. The 5 failures are pre-existing environment-drift issues (xgboost 3.2 API, lightgbm 4.6 warning, torch 2.12 tensor shape drift in NBEATS / DeepEnsemble, numpy 2.0+ np.float32 in test_lstm) unrelated to W5.1. The W3.5 status file documented 6 such pre-existing failures at the W3 batch exit; the W5.1 changes are additive and do not introduce new failures.

deviation_from_spec.prd_required_vs_default: The PRD W5.1 spec says 'required loss_fn parameter (Literal[...])' but the same spec note says 'the previous default cross_entropy is preserved for backward compat at the cost of explicitness per plan §5.1 + Critic minor #5'. The implementation honours the Critic round 1 minor #5 resolution: loss_fn is typed as a Literal (not Optional[str]) with a default value of 'cross_entropy' at the signature level. The Literal IS the load-bearing static contract; the default makes the trainer backward-compat for the W3.x call sites. W5.2 / W5.3 callers can pass loss_fn='sharpe' / 'cost_focal' explicitly; the W5.gate config (configs/models/default.yaml) will set the default-loss at the config layer, not at the function signature.
deviation_from_spec.tracker_param_key_choice: The PRD says 'mlflow run's params include loss_fn=cost_focal'. The implementation reports loss_fn as a top-level tracker param (alongside backend). This is the load-bearing channel; downstream mlflow queries can pivot on it. FoldMetrics.extras is not used because the trainer already aggregates loss_fn in TrainResult.extras (a single source of truth).
deviation_from_spec.deep_learning_backends_unchanged: The W5.1 scope explicitly excludes the actual sharpe / cost_focal implementations (those are W5.2 and W5.3). The deep-learning backends (lstm, nbeats, deep_ensemble's MLP) gain a loss_fn: str kwarg on _fit_core and treat it as advisory metadata — they continue to fit cross-entropy. The W5.2 / W5.3 release will replace the local 'criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()' line with a select-on-loss_fn call when the parallel agent's losses.py module is in place.

notes: W5.1 ships the loss_fn plumbing end-to-end: Trainer.fit_walkforward accepts a Literal-typed loss_fn (default 'cross_entropy'), validates it, threads it into Model.fit, and reports it via the tracker and TrainResult.extras. The 4 new tests pin the default, the unknown-name rejection, the threading into model.fit, and the tracker reporting. Pyright strict 0 errors. The W5.2 / W5.3 release (parallel agent) only needs to (a) add kairon/models/losses.py with SharpeLoss and CostFocalLoss torch modules, (b) replace the hard-coded nn.CrossEntropyLoss() in lstm.py / nbeats.py / deep_ensemble.py with a select-on-loss_fn call. No further signature changes are required — the W5.1 plumbing is the load-bearing contract for the W5 batch.
